Gflm}:lnexerdseoffirepowersmnfenedbysecflonsSandZSofme Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 (29 of 1986), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, namely: - - i {1} These rues may be called the Environment (Protection) Third Amendment Rules, 2005. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette or otherwise as mentioned. 2. 1n the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, in Schedule 1- (i) in serial number 14 relating to "Small Pulp and Paper Indusury’, in columns 3 and 4, after the existing entries, the - following entries shall be inserted, namely:- . - Sr. No. Industry Parameter Standards 1 2 3 4 ~Absorbable Organic Halogens ~ 3.00 kg/ton of paper (AOX) in effluent discharge produced with effect from the date of publication of this notification. Explanation.- These standards shall apply to all smal scale Pulp and Paper Mills having capacity below 24,000 MT per annum.”; -- 7 of 13 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY Paorlisec. 3] (i) - ;n serial number 53 relating to ' Large Pulp & Paper News| Print/Rayon Gradq Plants of capacity above 24,000 MT/Annum'- : (@ in column 2, for the words, figures and letfers " capacity “capacity above 24,000 MT per annum” shall be (b) in columns 3and 4, for " TOCL 2.0 kg/ton of| following shallbe substituted, namely:- above 24,000 MT/Annum", the words, figures and letters substitited; product”, the [ 2 ] 3 1 4 (i) paragreph C and entries relating thereto, the following shall - § ' namely:- ’ " C. Guidelines fof Disposal of Solid Waste, Drill Cutting and (AOX) In effluent discharge with of publi notification. 1.0 kgfton effect from in seriai number 72 relating io 'Ol Drililng and Gas Extraction for Offshore and Onshore Drilling Operation- 1. - Disposal of Drill Cutting and Drilling Fluids for On-shore “Absorbable Organic Halogens 1.5 kg/ton i product (a) Drill Cuttings (DC) originating from on-sh designed pit fined with impervious liner lo on-site. The disposal pit should be provi with leachate collection system. industry to State Pollution Control Board time of obtaining consent. from the date ion of this pf product with the 1% day of March, 2008."; Drilling Fluids nstallations: or locations dose to shore line and separated from Water Base Mud (WBM) should be properly washed and unusable drilling fluids (DF) such as WBM, Oll Base Mud (QBM), Synthetic Base Mud (SBM) should be disposed [off in a well off-site or addttionally . Design aspects of the impervious waste disposal pit; capping of disposal pit should be informed by the ol (SPCB) at the -- 8 of 13 -- L I—awE3()) A AT : SRS () © (@ (e) ® (G} o ()] (0] @ ® Use of ‘diesel base mud is prohibited. Only WBM should be used for on-shore oil drilling operations. In case of any problem due to geological formation for drilling, low toxicity OBM having aromatic content< 1% should be used. If the operators intend to use such OBM to mitigate specific whole problem/ SBM it should be intimated to Ministry of Environment and Forests/State Poliution Control Board. The chemical additives used for the preparation of DF should have low toxicity i.e. 96 hr LCs > 30,000 mg/l as per mysid toxicity or toxicity test conducted on locally available sensitive sea species. The chemicals used {mainly organic constituents) should be biodegradable. DC separated from OBM after washing should have oil contentat < 10 gm/kg for disposal into disposal pit. The waste pit after it is filled up shall be covered with impervious liner, over which, a thick layer of native soil with proper top slope is provided. Low toxicity OBM sheuld he made available at installation during driliing operation. Driling wastewater including DC wash water should be collected in the disposal pit evaporated or treated and should comply with the notified standards for on-shore disposal. Barite used in preparation of DF shall not contain Hg> 1 mg/kg & Cd> 3mg/kg. Total matenial acquired for preparation of drill site must be restored after completion of drilling operation leaving no waste material at site. SPCB should be informed about the restoration work, In case, environmentally acceptable methods for disposal of drill waste such as (a) injection to a formation through casing annuals, if conditions allow (b) land farming at suitable location (c) bio-remediation (d) incineration or (e) solidification can be considered, in such cases oit 2583 G [)reos-3 -- 9 of 13 -- ) mmmmmmm PaprII—Ssc. 3(1)] - Induwflsrequi'edms;bmltpropoal lo Ministry of ‘ Environment and Forests/State Pollution fontrol Board i (MOEF/SPCB) for approval. 2. Disposal. of Drill Cutting and Driling Fluids for Off-shore b (@) Use of diesel base mud is prohlbited. Only WBM is i permitted for offshore drilling. If the ope intend to use low toxicity OBM or SBM to mitigatel specific hole problems: in the formation, it should be| intimated to MOEF/SPCB. The low toxicity OBM should aromatic ‘ content<-1%. i (b) The toxidty of chemical additives used in DF (WBM ; or OBM or SBM) should be biodegradable (mainly organic constituents) and should have toxicity of 96 hr LC. 50 Value > 30,000 mg /I as per mysid toxigity or toxicity test conducted on locally avallable sensitive sea species. () - Hexavalent chromium compound should nét be used in DF. Aiternative chemicai in place of chrome Jignosuifonate td be uced In DF, In cace, chroma|compound i o SAOMC D¢ used in DF, In cage, chroma|compound | ' used, the DFf DCshoud not be disposed n:r:e (d) Bulk discharge of DF in offshore Is prohib emergency situations. (e) WBM/OBM /SBM should be recycled to|a maximum - extent, Unusable portion of OBM should not be discharged Into sea and shall be brought tq on-shore for . treatment & disposal in an impervious wastq disposal pit. () Thoroughly washed DC separated from WBM/SBM & unusable ‘portion of WBM/SBM having of 96 hr LCso > 30,000 mg/i shall be discharged off- into sea Intermittently, at an average rate of 50 bbi/hr/well from a platform so as to have proper dilution |& dispersion ‘without any adverse Impact on marine envirgnment. (@) bflll cutting of any compésition should not be discharged : in sensitive areas notified by the Ministry of Environment -- 10 of 13 -- [y I—JoE 3()1 D GO ;A (h) !nwseofspedficholeproblem,useofOBMwulbe restricied with zero discharge of DC.' Zero discharge would indude re-injection of the DC into a suitable formafionorwbringhoshoreforpmperdsposal.lns\m a case, use of OBM for re-injection should be recorded and made available to the regulatory agency. “Such low toxic OBM having aromatic content < 1% should be made available at the installation. M Incase, DCIs associated with high oil content from hydrocarbon bearing formation, then disposal of DC should not have oll content > 10 gm/kg. () The DC wash water should be treated to confirm limits notified under EPA, before disposal into Sea. The treated effiuent shouid be monitored regularty. (k) Discharge of DC from the installation located within 5 km away from shore should ensure that there is ric adverse Impact on marine Eco-system and on the shore. If, adverse impact is observed, then the Industries have to bring the DC on-shore for disposal in an Impervious - waste disposal pit. () I any, environmentai friendly tedinuiogy Energes Wor substitution of DF and dispasal technology, it may be brought to the notice of MoEF and regulatory agendes. If the operator desires to adopt such environment friendly technology a prior approval from Ministry of Environment and Forests s required. (m) Barite used in preparation of DF shall not contain Hg> 1 mg/kg & Cd> 3 mg/kg. (n) Oil driling operators are required to record daily discharge of DC & DF to offshore and also to monitor _daily the effluent quality, “and submit the compliance " report once in every six-morith to Ministry of Environment and Forests."”; -- 11 of 13 -- V3 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Part 0—Ssc. 3(i)} (iv)| after serial number 96 and entries relating thereto, the fpliowing serial nuber and entries shall' be added atthe end, namely:- a1 2 | 3 I 4 1 : "97. Guidelines for Poliution Centrol in Ginning Mills- Measures for Noise Control (I) Creating separate soundproof enclosures |for the fans W the ginning area. (i) Keeping the fans outside the ginning room in separate endosures. (i) Roller gins may be covered by sound proof enclosures and use of pnéumatic feeding of raw cotton while suction of ginned cotton is introduced to considerably reduce the dust pollution level. Measures for Dust Control (i) The fugitve emission can be iargely fontrolled by " employing mechanical or pneumatic hanflling of raw material and ginned material through covered ducts and providing overhead hoods connected to ust through ducts and fliters; use of lifting platforms for bale formers. (i) The overhead hoods with exhaust arranggment can be . provided at: (a) The saw-ginning machine where manual handling to maintain proper feeding in the machine. -- 12 of 13 -- [vmI—a%33)] STRT AT ;AR (b) At the feeding paint of the roller ginning machine when manual feeding is carried out. ©) At the collection points of ginned cotton from saw ginning condenser”..
